issues.description: OpenAI state commit was still not fully failure-atomic across a PARSE failure: after the R1 fix, OpenAIBinding.request committed _response_id and _sent_upto immediately after resp.json() but BEFORE self._from_response(data). A malformed-but-JSON response (e.g. {'id':'resp_1','output':[None],'status':'completed'}) raised inside _from_response after the cursor had advanced, so retrying the same instance could still skip unsent items. Round 1's test only covered the HTTP-raise path, not a parse failure after json() returns.
issues.verificationMethod: gpt-5.4 cross-provider re-review of the R1 failure-atomicity fix; constructed a concrete malformed-but-JSON repro.
issues.suggestedTestOrCheck: A response that parses-fails after resp.json() succeeds must leave _sent_upto and _response_id unchanged; a null output item must not crash _from_response.
issues.resolution_notes: Reordered to parse-before-commit (parsed = self._from_response(data); THEN commit _response_id/_sent_upto; return parsed) and added a defensive `if not isinstance(item, dict): continue` in _from_response so a null output item is skipped. Tests: test_offset_not_advanced_on_parse_failure, test_from_response_skips_non_dict_output_items. R3 VERIFIED, no regression.
